subject: Sports
title: The 4-4-2: Football's Default Formation
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The 4-4-2: Football's Default Formation.

hook.text: Four defenders, four midfielders, two strikers. It's the formation most people picture when they think football.

buildup.text: The 4-4-2 became dominant in the 1960s because it offered balance between defense and attack.

discovery.text: Its simplicity means players understand their roles clearly—two banks of four protecting the goal.

twist.text: Modern football has largely moved beyond the 4-4-2. Pep Guardiola called it 'too predictable' in 2009.

climax.text: Yet underdogs still use it effectively. A well-drilled 4-4-2 can frustrate even the best teams.

punchline.text: Simple, balanced, and timeless—football's most trusted shape.

quiz.question: Why did the 4-4-2 formation become so popular?
options: It offers a natural balance between defense and attack
It was required by FIFA rules
